config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial, keputusan yang terlalu mengorbankan individu sering berujung pada ketidakadilan, terutama bagi kelompok kecil atau yang lemah. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa menghapus martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in the United States, individual rights are exactly what keep majorities, governments, and institutions from becoming abusive in the name of the common good. I do think society can sometimes limit individual freedom for things like public safety or disease control, but those limits need to be narrow, justified, and accountable. If we treat “society” as more important than the person too easily, it becomes a convenient excuse to trample minorities and unpopular voices.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu secara prioritas otomatis. Di Indonesia, saya sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan tindakan yang akhirnya merugikan orang kecil, kelompok minoritas, atau warga yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang benar ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menghapus hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa menindas warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea is dangerous because "the common good" is often the phrase people use when they want to silence dissent, punish minorities, or give the state too much power. I do think there are real cases where individual rights can be limited for public safety, but those limits should be exceptional, narrow, and subject to strong oversight. A healthy society is one that protects the public without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, frasa “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya akses kuasa, jadi kalau tidak dibatasi justru rawan disalahgunakan. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan hak, tetapi itu harus benar-benar sempit, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling gampang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, that kind of claim is dangerous because it gives governments and majorities too much room to justify almost anything as being for the common good, and history shows that minorities usually pay the price. I do think there are limited cases, like emergencies or disease control, where some rights can be restricted, but those restrictions have to be narrow, temporary, and accountable. The burden should always be on society to prove that limiting a person’s rights is truly necessary, not on the individual to justify why they deserve basic freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
